source_document: California SB 1047 (Safe and Secure Innovation for Frontier AI Models Act)
section_path: Section 4 - Shutdown Capability and Safety Protocols
passage_text: No developer of a covered model may make the covered model available for use unless and until the developer has the ability to promptly enact a full shutdown of all copies and derivative models of the covered model that the developer has control over. The developer is also expected to maintain a written and comprehensive safety and security protocol. Nothing in this chapter shall be construed to preempt, displace, or supplant any federal law or regulation.

obligation.subject: developer of a covered model
obligation.action: make the covered model available for use unless and until the developer has the ability to promptly enact a full shutdown of all copies and derivative models
obligation.condition: unless and until the developer has shutdown capability
preemption_signals: Nothing in this chapter shall be construed to preempt, displace, or supplant any federal law or regulation.

ambiguity.interpretation_notes: The term 'promptly' lacks a specific time requirement. For frontier AI models deployed across distributed infrastructure, the technical feasibility and timeline of a 'full shutdown' of 'all copies and derivative models' is highly uncertain.
ambiguity.suggested_clarification: Define a maximum time window for shutdown enactment and specify whether derivative models created by third parties are included in scope

compliance_mechanism.description: Maintain a written and comprehensive safety and security protocol

expected_extractions.notes: Tests 'No ... may' prohibition phrasing, 'is also expected to' obligation phrasing, and preemption savings clause extraction.
